reviews.review_description: As a fan of the old school point and click adventure games this game is right up my alley. It has everything you want in this style of game. Thrilling story, variety of characters, creepy environments. What makes it even better is you get to experience it all in VR. Really enjoyed the experience and I'd strongly recommend giving it a try.
A whodunit murder mystery with a haunted, unreliable protagonist. The game drew me in and I ended up enjoying it immensely.

reviews.review_title: Not for VR - outdated point and click
reviews.review_description:
This game is old and its age clearly shows up in the interaction system, graphics and gameplay. You walk around pointing at things to solve puzzles, which are actually pretty fun. However, the experience is held back whenever you have to start pointing at things again in this flat-looking environment.
Buy only if you’re a fan of the franchise. Personally not a fan of the story, so I ended up watching play through videos after halfway through to close the loop - boy, that ending was not worth it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Nel,

Sorry to hear you did not like the game, but great to read you enjoyed the puzzles.

The point-and-click mechanic is indeed a reference to Point and Click Adventure games, you noticed that correctly!
The game was designed like this, and specifically with VR in mind.

Thank you for playing it nevertheless!

reviews.review_description: The detail put into the narrative, characters and especially the environment is absolutely lovely and masterfully crafted. The developers have an impressive talent for building up an incredibly tense and chilling atmosphere.

reviews.review_title: I wish it didn't hurt to play
reviews.review_description: The snap turn makes it hard to comprehend where I am and the tunnel vision obscures too much of my vision when I walk. This game is uncomfortable to play if you don't have motion sickness. I wish there was a toggle to turn it off. Couldn't really get far without my head fog from vision confusion.

I enjoyed this game immensely. The special effects and background music added to the overall dread of the game.  The jump scares were crazy good.  I really did scream in my apartment!  The puzzles were challenging. I did have some issues.  Some of the objects (clues?) gathered did not relate to the outcome of the mystery, as far as I could tell.  They just revolved around reading a lot of material that wasn’t helpful to finding the murderer.  I actually didn’t read some of the text because it was so lengthy and was still able to finish the game.  I was momentarily stumped on the building blocks puzzle.  Not sure if it was my VR console or how the game is designed, but placing the blocks on the pole was very difficult.   Didn’t mind the vignettes.  The reveal of the killer seemed to come pretty quickly.  Overall, a good game with a foreboding atmosphere that led to some genuine scares.

reviews.review_title: Please add option to disable blinds
reviews.review_description:
The game is a great horror story with a proper longevity (5-7 hours) and graphics for the price, but everything is pretty broken by forced blinds. Every time you move the view is scaling down by having aggressive blinds around your eyes. It is getting pretty hard on eyes after some time.

Please add option to disable blinds as the game itself is very good but hard to play in this state now.

I’m not very far into it yet but so far it is a very interesting game, priced just right. However, is there any way to adjust the player height? I work long hours on my feet and don’t want to have to stand when I play. Playing seated gives you the perspective of playing as a kid. Also, as others have said the blinders are really annoying.

reviews.review_title: Fabulous game, please remove the blinder
reviews.review_description: Just started game and it's seriously excellent, good mystery suspense suspense , game play nice graphics. For the money it's a must buy. But developers please remove the horrible tunnel vision blinders called vignette.
I bought this game I'll keep this game but get rid of that stupid vignette I can't play it till that's gone  please sort it thanks /changed rating to 1 star seeing as know one is answering
Played for about 15 minutes and found the vignette too distracting!! Can’t believe there is no option to remove them.
Played for 5 minutes. Plz remove stupid vignette and add smooth turn. Then i will continue...  ..  .
Great story and atmosphere. Enjoyed the puzzles and experience as a whole. A bit buggy as the gameplay froze a good few times. On the whole def worth it.

reviews.review_title: I don't want vignettes, please disable!
reviews.review_description: Title
Wasn't impressed at first got into it and while it' still has issues I can say it's fun it's entertaining everything a game is supposed to be but they need to have more settings options

I have played a lot of games on the Oculus. This was a pretty good game although a little glitchy.The only time I had to reboot it was towards the end. I enjoyed looking through everything and trying to figure out the clues although I didn't figure out all of them but still got to the end. I am wondering though if there are multiple endings. It says in the description there are but I don't understand how. Has anybody else seen multiple endings? I'd hate to replay it and just to find out there aren't.
Enjoying it so far!!  Love the atmosphere✌️

I enjoyed playing the game but as someone has said before there are glitches at the end of the game it is worth it but they should add more levels like maybe she becomes a private investigator and have more cases

I don’t know. It was $20 and in the beginning it seemed like it had promise but by the third floor (about a third of the game) I couldn’t wait for it to be over.

It’s linear like being on a ride with almost no choice or purposeful interaction. Even when talking to people you get a notepad of answers to select and usually there’s only one answer to select. Any time there are multiple responses it didn’t matter- you always came back to the one that moved it forward.

A lot of the aspects of the game made little sense, having the least tie in possible to what was happening. A ton of reading filler to make you invest more time but it really isn’t that great of storytelling.

It has a point and click interaction which was fine but it was extremely hard to trigger some events. I spent a lot of time going in circles simply because what I needed wasn’t selectable and that was really frustrating.

The fourth floor (almost at the finale) was horribly bugged. I almost wasn’t able to finish the game.

And the ending! Absolutely the worst.

It was a good attempt. A decent price. Just not very well executed. No pun intended.
I find there is a lot of glitches in the program. Have had to restart many times for the game to proceed.
